info.description: This is the backend API for a conference ticketing system.
info.title: Multi-Track Ticketing API

get.description: Returns a paginated cross-tenant list of events with optional filters and sorting. Requires system_admin role.

parameters.description: Filter by status: active, past, or all (default all)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Case-insensitive search on event name, event_code, or description
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Case-insensitive search on owner email, name, or last_name
parameters.name: owner_search

parameters.description: true: owner_id set; false: no owner
parameters.name: has_owner

parameters.description: Sort field: created_at (default), updated_at, start_date, end_date, name, event_code, owner_email
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Sort order: asc or desc (default desc)
parameters.name: order

get.description: Returns time-bucketed events (by start calendar day, multi-day events repeat each day) with registration, check-in, and sent-invitation counts. Requires system_admin role.

parameters.description: Bucket size: day (default). week and month are not yet supported.
parameters.name: granularity

parameters.description: Filter events: active, past, or all (default all)
parameters.name: status

get.description: Returns cross-tenant event detail including schedule, owner, attendee counts, team, deliverables, and tiers. Requires system_admin role.
Returns ok when the caller is authenticated and has the system_admin role. Used to verify platform-admin access.
Returns paginated social networks enabled for user-generated content on the platform. Requires system_admin role.

post.description: Adds a social network to the platform UGC catalog. Code must be unique (e.g. instagram, linkedin). Requires system_admin role.
post.summary: Add a UGC social network

delete.description: Removes a social network from the platform UGC catalog by code. Requires system_admin role.
delete.summary: Remove a UGC social network

get.description: Returns the list of events the authenticated user is registered for, including registration metadata. Supports filtering by status (active, past, or all) and offset-based pagination.
get.summary: Get events the current user is registered for

parameters.description: Filter by status: active, past, or all (default all)
parameters.name: status

post.summary: Issue short-lived JWT for agenda WebSocket (when Bearer header unavailable)

get.description: Returns DM threads for the caller with the latest message preview per conversation.
get.summary: List DM conversation inbox for an event

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from previous response next_cursor
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Returns paginated DM messages oldest-first within each page. Caller must be registered for the event.
get.summary: List direct message history with another attendee

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from previous response next_cursor
parameters.name: cursor

post.description: Posts a 1:1 DM within the event. Optional reply_to_message_id quotes a prior message in the same DM thread. Both users must be registered for the event.
post.summary: Send a direct message to another attendee

get.description: Returns paginated general chat messages oldest-first within each page. Caller must be registered for the event.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from previous response next_cursor
parameters.name: cursor

post.description: Posts a message to the event general chat room. Optional reply_to_message_id quotes a prior general message in the same event. Caller must be registered for the event.
post.summary: Send a general event chat message

delete.description: Soft-deletes a general or DM message. Only the sender may delete. Idempotent: repeat delete returns 204 without re-broadcasting.
delete.summary: Delete a chat message sent by the caller

put.description: Adds or replaces the caller's single emoji reaction on a general or DM message. Idempotent when the same emoji is set again (no WebSocket re-broadcast).
put.summary: Set or replace emoji reaction on a chat message

200.description: data contains message_id and aggregated reactions

delete.description: Removes the caller's reaction if present. Idempotent when no reaction exists.
delete.summary: Remove caller's emoji reaction from a chat message

200.description: data contains message_id and aggregated reactions

post.description: Records the authenticated user's check-in to the event. The user must be registered for the event. Idempotent: returns 201 when a new check-in is created, 200 when already checked in.
post.summary: Self-check-in to an event

get.description: Returns the event name, attendee identity fields (name, last name, email), and the attendee's assigned tier (name and optional color) for the specified event. Caller must be registered for the event.
Returns read-only feature toggles for a registered attendee.

Returns paginated networking profiles for registered attendees who set profile_is_public=true. Caller must be registered for the event. Email is omitted from profiles. Supports optional search (name, headline, or company), interest/offering filters, and sort.
get.summary: List public attendee profiles for an event

parameters.description: Substring match on name, headline, or company
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Filter by topics of interest (OR match; repeat param for multiple values)
parameters.name: interest

parameters.description: Filter by intent offering (OR match; repeat param for multiple values)
parameters.name: offering

get.description: Returns distinct interests and offerings from visible public profiles in the event, plus allowed sort and order values. Caller must be registered for the event.
get.summary: Get public profile search filter options for an event

get.description: Returns a single public networking profile for a registered attendee with profile_is_public=true. Caller must be registered for the event. Returns 404 when the profile is not public or the user is not registered.
get.summary: Get a public attendee profile for an event

post.description: Registers the authenticated user as an attendee for the specified event. Idempotent: returns 201 when a new registration is created, 200 when already registered.
post.summary: Register the current attendee for an event

get.description: Returns all reviews created by the authenticated attendee for sessions within the specified event. The user must be registered for the event.
get.summary: List my session reviews for an event

get.description: Returns the event schedule (event plus rooms that have relevant sessions) for the specified event. Only registered attendees may access this. A room appears when the attendee has a booking there or it has an all_attend session; all_attend sessions are included even in not_bookable rooms. Sessions shown are the attendee's bookings plus every all_attend session (visible statuses only).
get.summary: Get event schedule for a registered attendee

200.description: data contains event and rooms with nested sessions (booked + all_attend, including all_attend in not_bookable rooms)

get.description: Returns the booked sessions for the authenticated attendee in the specified event, ordered by session start time. Only registered attendees or the event owner may access this.

200.description: data contains attendee bookings ordered by session start time

post.description: Records the authenticated user's check-in to a live session identified by per-event session_number. User must be registered. Idempotent: returns 201 when a new check-in is created, 200 when already checked in.
post.summary: Self-check-in to a session by session number

get.description: Returns session IDs the authenticated attendee has checked into within the specified event. The user must be registered for the event.
get.summary: List my session check-in IDs for an event

200.description: data contains session IDs ordered by check-in time

post.description: Books the given session for the authenticated attendee. The user must be registered for the event. If the session overlaps with other booked sessions, returns 409 unless override=true (then removes overlapping bookings and books this session). Room capacity is enforced. New bookings are not allowed while the session is Live. Idempotent: returns 200 when already booked.
post.summary: Book a session for the current attendee

parameters.description: If true, remove overlapping bookings and book this session (default false)
parameters.name: override

400.description: error.code: missing_path_param | invalid_path_param | invalid_query_param | invalid_request_body | session_all_attend

delete.description: Removes the authenticated attendee's booking for the given session. The user must be registered for the event; the session must belong to the event. Idempotent: returns 204 when the booking was removed or when no booking existed.
delete.summary: Cancel a session booking for the current attendee

204.description: No Content - booking cancelled or already absent

get.description: Returns the authenticated attendee's review for the given session. The user must be registered for the event and checked in to the session.
get.summary: Get a review for a checked-in session

post.description: Creates the authenticated attendee's review for the given session. The user must be registered for the event and checked in to the session.
post.summary: Create a review for a checked-in session

delete.description: Deletes the authenticated attendee's review for the given session. The user must be registered for the event and checked in to the session. Idempotent: returns 204 when deleted or already absent.
delete.summary: Delete a review for a checked-in session

204.description: No Content - review deleted or already absent

patch.description: Updates the authenticated attendee's review for the given session. The user must be registered for the event and checked in to the session.
patch.summary: Update a review for a checked-in session

get.description: Returns a paginated list of published sponsors for registered attendees. Requires sponsors feature enabled. Only published sponsors are returned.

parameters.description: Search name, tagline, or booth label
parameters.name: search

get.description: Returns a published sponsor with nested published offerings. Records a profile_view engagement server-side. Requires sponsors feature enabled and event registration.

200.description: data contains the sponsor with nested published offerings

post.description: Records an append-only sponsor engagement event. action is required. offering_id is required for offering_view and offering_click; must be omitted for list_impression, profile_view, and favorite. profile_view is recorded automatically on sponsor detail GET — clients should not duplicate. booth_check_in is not supported (returns 400). Idempotent when idempotency_key is provided (max 128 chars). Requires sponsors feature enabled and event registration.
post.summary: Record sponsor engagement (attendee)

get.description: Returns published offerings for a published sponsor as a JSON array in data. Requires sponsors feature enabled and event registration.

200.description: data is an array of published offerings

post.description: Registers the authenticated user as an attendee for the event with the given event_code. Idempotent: returns 201 when a new registration is created, 200 when already registered.
post.summary: Register for an event by event code

post.description: Send a one-time login code to the given email. The code expires after a short period.
post.summary: Request a login code

post.description: Exchange the one-time code for a JWT and user. Creates the user on first successful login. Returns token, token_type, and user.
post.summary: Verify login code and get token

200.description: data contains token, token_type, and user

post.description: Create a new conference event. Name and start_date (YYYY-MM-DD) are required; duration_days defaults to 1 if omitted; description, location_lat, location_lng are optional. organization_id is required when the user already belongs to an organization; omitted on first event auto-creates "My Organization". Id, event_code and timestamps are server-generated. The authenticated user becomes the event owner.
post.summary: Create a new event

parameters.description: Event data: name and start_date required; duration_days optional (defaults to 1); description and location optional
parameters.name: event

get.description: Returns events where the authenticated user is the owner or a team member (managed events). Requires Bearer token.
get.summary: List events managed by the current user

200.description: data is an array of events

get.description: Returns the event with its rooms, each room containing nested sessions. Requires authentication.

200.description: data contains event and rooms with nested sessions

delete.description: Delete an event and all its associated data (rooms, sessions). Only the event owner can delete. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Delete an event

patch.description: Updates event date, description, and location (lat/lng). Only the event owner can update. Optional fields omitted from body are unchanged. Requires authentication.

post.description: Same ticket shape as attendee issuance; caller must be event owner or team member. Use when browser cannot send `Authorization` on WebSocket handshake (`GET /ws?ticket=...`).
post.summary: Issue short-lived JWT for agenda WebSocket (organizer app)

get.description: Returns aggregate metrics (registrations, check-ins, invitation funnel, deliverables, per-session occupancy) for a past event. Each session includes check_in_count, booking_count, overflow_count, optional capacity_utilization (check_in_count / room.capacity, may exceed 1.0), and optional overflow_rate (overflow_count / check_in_count). Caller must be the event owner or a team member. Available only after the event has ended.
get.summary: Get post-event analytics for an event

200.description: data contains aggregate metrics and per-session occupancy

get.description: Returns time-bucketed event check-in counts across all event calendar days (hourly or 15-minute buckets in the event time zone). Caller must be the event owner or a team member. Available only after the event has ended.
get.summary: Get check-in time series for post-event analytics

get.description: Returns a nodes/links graph of attendee movement through the event: event check-in, session check-ins in chronological order, and early drop-off. Each node is a stage (check_in, a session, or drop_off) and each link is a directed transition weighted by attendee count. Caller must be the event owner or a team member.
get.summary: Get attendee flow (Sankey) graph for an event

get.description: Returns a paginated list of announcements sent for the event. Only the event owner or team members can list. Use page and page_size query params.

post.description: Creates an announcement and sends push notifications to all attendees checked in to the event. Only the event owner or team members can send. Push delivery is best-effort per recipient; the announcement is always persisted. Actions: info (no navigation), open_event, open_session (requires session_id), open_agenda, open_url (requires https url).
post.summary: Send a push announcement to checked-in attendees

get.description: Returns the event with rooms and nested sessions for attendee views. Rooms with not_bookable=true are excluded. Requires authentication.
get.summary: Get attendee-facing event schedule by event ID

200.description: data contains event and bookable rooms with nested sessions

get.description: Returns paginated chat bans for organizer moderation. Event owner or team member only.
get.summary: List chat-banned attendees for an event

post.description: Bans a registered attendee from sending chat messages and hides them from public profiles. Event owner or team member only. Idempotent when already banned.
post.summary: Ban an attendee from event chat

delete.description: Removes a chat ban for a registered attendee. Event owner or team member only.
delete.summary: Unban an attendee from event chat

delete.description: Soft-deletes a general chat message. Event owner or team member only. DM messages cannot be deleted via this endpoint. Idempotent: repeat delete returns 204 without re-broadcasting.
delete.summary: Delete a general chat message as event organizer

get.description: Returns paginated organizers chat messages oldest-first within each page. Event owner or team member only; registration not required.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from previous response next_cursor
parameters.name: cursor

post.description: Posts a message to the backstage organizers chat room. Optional reply_to_message_id quotes a prior organizers message in the same event. Event owner or team member only; registration not required.
post.summary: Send an organizers-only event chat message

delete.description: Soft-deletes a message the caller sent in the organizers chat room. Sender only. Event owner or team member only; registration not required. Idempotent: repeat delete returns 204 without re-broadcasting.
delete.summary: Delete own message in organizers chat

put.description: Adds or replaces the caller's single emoji reaction on an organizers chat message. Event owner or team member only.
put.summary: Set or replace emoji reaction on an organizers chat message

200.description: data contains message_id and aggregated reactions

delete.description: Removes the caller's reaction if present. Event owner or team member only.
delete.summary: Remove caller's emoji reaction from an organizers chat message

200.description: data contains message_id and aggregated reactions

post.description: Records check-in of an attendee for the event. Caller must be the event owner or a team member. If the user is not registered for the event, they are auto-registered first. Idempotent: returns 201 when a new check-in is created, 200 when already checked in.
post.summary: Check in an attendee (owner or team member)

parameters.description: Attendee user ID to check in
parameters.name: body

get.description: Generates a Bevy-compatible CSV file containing checked-in attendees for an event. Caller must be the event owner or a team member.
Returns a paginated list of event deliverables for event managers (owner or team members).

post.description: Creates a deliverable that event managers (owner or team members) can give to attendees.
post.summary: Add a deliverable to an event

delete.description: Deletes an event deliverable for event managers (owner or team members).
delete.summary: Delete an event deliverable

patch.description: Updates mutable fields of an event deliverable for event managers (owner or team members).

post.description: Records that a manager gave a deliverable to an attendee. The attendee must be registered and checked in to the event; if not, returns 422 (error.code: unprocessable_entity). If already given, returns 409 unless the deliverable is repeatable and give_anyway is true.
post.summary: Give a deliverable to an attendee

post.description: Import rooms and sessions from Sessionize for a specific event
post.summary: Import schedule from Sessionize

get.description: Returns a paginated list of emails invited to the event (with id and sent_at). Only the event owner can list. Use page and page_size query params. Optional search filters by email substring (case-insensitive). Requires authentication.
get.summary: List invited emails for an event

post.description: Enqueue invitation emails for asynchronous delivery. Body contains a string of emails separated by commas or spaces. Only the event owner can invite. Each invitation is persisted immediately; a background worker then sends via SES, retrying on transient errors and persisting the error on terminal failures so nothing is silently dropped. Returns the count of invitations queued and the list of emails rejected (duplicates for this event or storage errors). Observe final delivery status via GET /events/{eventID}/invitations.
post.summary: Enqueue event invitation emails

200.description: data contains queued count and rejected list

get.description:
**Pre-stream errors** use the normal JSON envelope (401/403/404/500) before `Content-Type: text/event-stream` is set.

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer` or `?ticket=` JWT from `POST /events/{eventID}/live/status/ticket`. When using `ticket`, its `event_id` claim must match `{eventID}` in the path or the server responds 403.

**Stream body:** not `APIResponse`. Each frame is `event:` then `data:` (single JSON object) then blank line.

**Event types:**
- `snapshot` — `data` is `EventStatusLiveSnapshotSSEData` (see schema): `event_check_in_count`, `sessions` (**live-only**; each session has `check_in_count`, `booking_count`, `overflow_count`, optional `capacity_utilization`, optional `overflow_rate`, and `room`), `deliverables`.
- `error` — `data` is `{code, message}`; connection ends after this frame.

**Heartbeats:** comment lines `: ping` every ~20s; ignore (no `event`/`data`).

**First frame:** immediate `snapshot` after connect (same shape as above).

**Reconnect:** clients may reconnect with exponential backoff; server closes stream on shutdown.

**Ops:** place stream path behind `proxy_buffering off` and a large `proxy_read_timeout` if using nginx.
get.summary: SSE stream of event check-in and deliverable stats

200.description: JSON shape of each snapshot event data line (response Content-Type is text/event-stream)

post.description: Returns a JWT usable as `?ticket=` on `GET /events/{eventID}/live/status/stream` for EventSource (cannot send Authorization). Caller must be event owner or team member. Ticket TTL is at most 60 seconds.
post.summary: Issue short-lived SSE stream ticket

get.description: Returns a paginated list of user registrations for the event (registration + user info). Each item includes checked_in and may include tier (assigned event tier) when present. Only the event owner or team members can list. Use page and page_size query params. Optional search filters by attendee name or email (case-insensitive). Requires authentication.

parameters.description: Filter attendees by name or email substring (case-insensitive)
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Filter attendees by assigned event tier id (UUID)
parameters.name: tier_id

get.description: Returns a paginated list of attendee session reviews for the event (review + session + reviewer info). Only the event owner or team members can list. Use page and page_size query params. Optional session_id, rating (1-5), and search (comment substring, case-insensitive) filters. Requires authentication.
get.summary: List session reviews for an event

get.description: Returns the list of rooms for the event. Only the event owner can list. Requires authentication.

200.description: data is an array of rooms

post.description: Creates a new room for the event. Only the event owner can create. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Create a room

get.description: Returns a single room for the event. Only the event owner can access. Requires authentication.

delete.description: Deletes a room and its sessions. Only the event owner can delete. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Delete a room

patch.description: Updates room details (name, capacity, description, how_to_get_there, not_bookable). Only the event owner can update. Optional fields omitted from body are unchanged (name and not_bookable keep current value when omitted). Requires authentication.
Toggles the not_bookable flag for a room. Only the event owner can toggle. Requires authentication.

get.description: Returns the list of tiers allowed to book/check-in this room. Only the event manager can access. Requires authentication.
get.summary: List allowed tiers for a room

200.description: data is an array of event tiers

post.description: Links an event tier to a room so that attendees with that tier can book/check-in. Only the event manager can add. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Allow a tier to book/check-in a room

delete.description: Removes the tier from the room so that attendees with that tier can no longer book/check-in. Only the event manager can remove. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Remove an allowed tier from a room

get.description: Returns sessions for the event on the current page: scheduled sessions grouped by room (with full room details) and draft/unscheduled sessions. Only event owner or team members may call. Optional search matches title or description (case-insensitive). Use page and page_size query params.
get.summary: List event sessions grouped by room (paginated)

parameters.description: Filter sessions by title or description substring (case-insensitive)
parameters.name: search

200.description: data contains rooms, unscheduled sessions, and pagination

post.description: Creates a new session for the event in a given room and time slot, with optional tags and speakers. Only the event owner can create. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Create a session

get.description: Returns paginated sessions with no room assigned for the event. Same access as the full sessions schedule list: event owner or team members only. Optional search matches title or description (case-insensitive). Use page and page_size query params.
get.summary: List unscheduled (draft) sessions for an event

parameters.description: Filter by title or description substring (case-insensitive)
parameters.name: search

delete.description: Deletes a session. Only the event owner can delete. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Delete a session

patch.description: Moves a session to a different room and/or time slot by updating room_id, start_time, and end_time. Only the event owner can update. Optional fields omitted from body are unchanged. Requires authentication.

post.description: Toggles whether a session should appear on all attendees' schedules (registered attendees). Only event managers can toggle.
post.summary: Toggle session all-attend flag

delete.description: Deletes all session bookings for users who have not checked in to the session. Caller must be the event owner or a team member. Frees capacity for others to book or check in. Returns the number of bookings released.
delete.summary: Release unchecked-in session bookings (owner or team member)

post.description: Records check-in of an attendee to a specific session. Caller must be the event owner or a team member. User must be registered for the event. Session must be Live. If the user has no booking and the session has capacity, they are auto-booked then checked in. When override_capacity is true and the session is full with no booking, records an overflow check-in (overflow=true) without creating a booking; unchecked-in bookings must be released first via DELETE .../bookings. If the session is not Live or is full and the user has no booking and override_capacity is false, returns 409. Idempotent: returns 201 when a new check-in is created, 200 when already checked in.
post.summary: Check in an attendee to a session (owner or team member)

parameters.description: Attendee user ID to check in
parameters.name: body

patch.description: Updates a session's title, description, and/or technical_difficulty. Only the event owner can update. Optional fields omitted from body are unchanged. Requires authentication.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of attendee reviews for a single session (review + session + reviewer info). Only the event owner or team members can list. Use page and page_size query params. Optional rating (1-5) and search (comment substring, case-insensitive) filters. Requires authentication.
get.summary: List session reviews for a session

get.description: Returns the list of speakers for the session (full speaker objects). Only the event owner can list. Requires authentication.

200.description: data is an array of speakers

post.description: Links a speaker (by id) to a session. The speaker must already belong to the event. Only the event owner can add. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Add a speaker to a session

delete.description: Unlinks a speaker from a session. Only the event owner can remove. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Remove a speaker from a session

patch.description: Updates a session's lifecycle status. Only event managers can update. When setting a session to Live, only one Live session is allowed per room.

post.description: Links a tag (by id) to a session. The tag must already belong to the event. Only the event owner can add. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Add a tag to a session

delete.description: Unlinks a tag from a session. Only the event owner can remove. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Remove a tag from a session

get.description: Returns the list of tiers allowed to book/check-in this session. Only the event manager can access. Requires authentication.
get.summary: List allowed tiers for a session

200.description: data is an array of event tiers

post.description: Links an event tier to a session so that attendees with that tier can book/check-in. Only the event manager can add. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Allow a tier to book/check-in a session

delete.description: Removes the tier from the session so that attendees with that tier can no longer book/check-in. Only the event manager can remove. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Remove an allowed tier from a session

get.description: Returns event feature toggles and UGC configuration for organizers.

patch.description: Partially updates event feature toggles and UGC social network selection. Only provided fields are changed.

get.description: Returns the list of speakers for the event. Only the event owner can list. Requires authentication.

200.description: data is an array of speakers

post.description: Creates a new speaker for the event (manual create). Only the event owner can create. Optional profile_picture accepts an S3 object key from POST .../speakers/profile-picture/upload-url; responses return a public URL. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Create a speaker

post.description: Returns a presigned URL and object key that the client can use to upload a speaker profile picture directly to storage before creating or updating a speaker. Pass the returned key in profile_picture on POST/PATCH speaker. Requires Bearer token. Only the event owner can call this.
post.summary: Get presigned upload URL for speaker profile picture

get.description: Returns a single speaker for the event with the list of sessions they speak in. Only the event owner can access. Requires authentication.

delete.description: Deletes a speaker. Session-speaker links are removed by cascade. Only the event owner can delete. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Delete a speaker

patch.description: Updates speaker details. Only the event owner can update. Optional fields omitted from body are unchanged. profile_picture accepts an S3 object key from POST .../speakers/profile-picture/upload-url; responses return a public URL. Requires authentication.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of sponsors for organizer management (all statuses). Only the event owner or team members can list.

parameters.description: Search name, tagline, or booth label
parameters.name: search

post.description: Creates a sponsor profile for the event. Only the event owner or team members can create sponsors.
post.summary: Create an event sponsor

get.description: Returns per-sponsor engagement aggregates and offering breakdown for organizer dashboards. Only the event owner or team members can view.
Returns a sponsor with nested offerings. Only the event owner or team members can get.

200.description: data contains the sponsor with nested offerings

delete.description: Deletes a sponsor and cascades offerings and engagements. Only the event owner or team members can delete.
delete.summary: Delete an event sponsor

patch.description: Partially updates a sponsor. Only the event owner or team members can update.

put.description: Stores the uploaded logo object key on the sponsor. Only the event owner or team members can confirm.

post.description: Returns a presigned URL for uploading a sponsor logo (JPEG, PNG, or WebP). Only the event owner or team members can request.
post.summary: Request sponsor logo upload URL

get.description: Returns all offerings for a sponsor as a JSON array in data (not wrapped in items). Only the event owner or team members can list.

200.description: data is an array of offerings

post.description: Creates an offering (CTA/perk) for a sponsor. title and kind are required. Use cta_url and cta_label for link, resource, and demo kinds; coupon_code for kind=coupon; swag is informational. Only the event owner or team members can create.
post.summary: Create a sponsor offering

delete.description: Deletes a sponsor offering. Only the event owner or team members can delete.
delete.summary: Delete a sponsor offering

patch.description: Partially updates a sponsor offering. Use cta_url and cta_label for link, resource, and demo kinds; coupon_code for kind=coupon. Only the event owner or team members can update.

get.description: Returns the list of tags associated with the event. Only the event owner can list. Requires authentication.

200.description: data is an array of tags

post.description: Adds one or more tags to the event by name (creates tags if missing). Only the event owner can add. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Add tags to an event

201.description: data contains the event's tags after add

delete.description: Removes the tag from the event and from all sessions of that event. Only the event owner can remove. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Remove a tag from an event

patch.description: Renames a tag that belongs to the event. Only the event owner can update. Requires authentication.

get.description: Returns the list of team members for the event. Only the event owner or team members can list. Requires authentication.
get.summary: List team members of an event

200.description: data is an array of team members

post.description: Add users as team members of the event by email. Body contains a string of emails separated by commas or spaces. Only the event owner can add. Creates attendee accounts for unknown emails when possible. Returns count of newly added members and list of failed addresses (owner, already member, or error).
post.summary: Add team members to an event by email

200.description: data contains added count and failed list

delete.description: Remove a user from the event's team members. Only the event owner can remove. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Remove a team member from an event

parameters.description: User ID (UUID) of the team member to remove
parameters.name: userID

put.description: Confirms the uploaded event thumbnail by storing the object key on the event and returning the updated event. Requires Bearer token. Only the event owner or team members can call this.

post.description: Returns a presigned URL and object key that the client can use to upload an event thumbnail directly to storage. Requires Bearer token. Only the event owner or team members can call this.
post.summary: Get presigned upload URL for event thumbnail

get.description: Returns the list of tiers for the event. Only the event owner can list. Requires authentication.

200.description: data is an array of event tiers

post.description: Creates a tier for the event with the given name. Only the event owner can create. Requires authentication.
post.summary: Create an event tier

delete.description: Deletes the tier from the event. Only the event owner can delete. Requires authentication.
delete.summary: Delete an event tier

patch.description: Updates the tier name. Only the event owner can update. Requires authentication.

parameters.description: New tier name and optional color (hex)
parameters.name: body

post.description: Assign users (by email) to the specified event tier. Body contains a string of emails separated by commas or spaces. Only the event owner or team members can assign. Users must already exist in the database. Duplicates for the same tier are skipped. Returns count of newly added and list of failed addresses (not found or error).
post.summary: Assign users by email to an event tier

200.description: data contains added count and failed list

get.description: Deprecated: use GET /events/{eventID}/settings instead. Returns UGC enabled state and selected social networks for the event. Only event managers (owner or team members) can read.

200.description: data contains enabled flag and social networks

put.description: Deprecated: use PATCH /events/{eventID}/settings instead. Sets whether UGC is enabled for the event and which platform social networks are active. social_network_codes must be provided (may be empty). Only event managers can update.

parameters.description: UGC enabled flag and social network codes
parameters.name: body

get.description: Returns paginated platform-wide social networks available for event UGC configuration. Only event managers (owner or team members) can read.
get.summary: List platform UGC social networks for event organizers

get.description: Returns minimum required and latest app versions/build numbers and store URLs for attendee/organizer apps. Clients should enforce forced update when their build number is below min_build.
get.summary: Get mobile remote configuration (minimum supported versions)

get.description: Returns organizations the caller belongs to, including their role in each.
get.summary: List organizations for the authenticated user

post.description: Creates a new organization; the authenticated user becomes owner.
post.summary: Create an organization

get.description: Returns organization details when the caller is a member.

patch.description: Renames an organization. Caller must be owner or admin.

get.description: Returns all events belonging to the organization for reporting. Caller must be a member.
Returns members of the organization. Caller must be a member.

post.description: Adds an existing user to the organization by email. Caller must be owner or admin.
post.summary: Add organization member

delete.description: Removes a member from the organization. Caller must be owner or admin. Cannot remove the last owner.
delete.summary: Remove organization member

patch.description: Changes a member's role. Caller must be organization owner.

get.description: Returns session details (including tags and speakers). Requires authentication.
Returns the authenticated user's profile (id, email, name, created_at, updated_at). Requires Bearer token.

delete.description: Closes and anonymizes the authenticated account (PII removed, deleted_at set). Registrations, team memberships, bookings, and check-ins keep the same user_id for consistent analytics. User roles and login codes for the old email are cleared; the email can be used for a new account. Rejected with conflict if the user owns an active (not yet past) event; past owned events are kept without an organizer. Subsequent requests with the same JWT receive 401 until a new login. Requires Bearer token.
delete.summary: Delete current user account

patch.description: Update the authenticated user's profile. Accepts optional name, last_name, profile_is_public, headline, company, linkedin_url, web_page_url, topics_of_interest, intent_seeking, and intent_offering; email cannot be updated. Requires Bearer token.

parameters.description: Fields to update (name and/or last_name, both optional)
parameters.name: body

put.description: Confirms the uploaded profile picture by storing the object key on the user and returning the updated user profile. Requires Bearer token.

post.description: Returns a presigned URL and object key that the client can use to upload the user's profile picture directly to storage. Requires Bearer token.
post.summary: Get presigned upload URL for profile picture

put.description: Upserts the caller's FCM registration token for a device and app. Call after login and on token refresh. Requires Bearer token.
put.summary: Register or refresh a device push token

delete.description: Removes the caller's stored push token for a device and app. Call on logout. Requires Bearer token.
delete.summary: Unregister a device push token

get.description: Returns paginated catalog items for a category (topic, intent_seeking, intent_offering). Optional search filters by name substring. Used for profile field autocomplete. Requires Bearer token.

post.description: Creates a catalog item for a category if it does not already exist (idempotent). Does not attach the item to the user profile; use PATCH /users/me to select it. Requires Bearer token.
post.summary: Create a profile catalog item

profile_picture.description: ProfilePicture is the S3 object key from POST .../speakers/profile-picture/upload-url (optional). Responses return a public URL.
ProfilePicture is the S3 object key from POST .../speakers/profile-picture/upload-url (optional). Responses return a public URL.

time_zone.description:
TimeZone is stored as a fixed UTC offset like "+02:00" or "-05:00".
Session/talk times imported from external sources are converted into this zone.

check_in_rate.description: CheckInRate is check_in_count / registration_count (0.0–1.0). Omitted when registration_count == 0.

check_ins_not_on_sent_invitations_count.description: CheckInsNotOnSentInvitationsCount is check-ins whose user email has no matching sent invitation.

check_ins_not_on_sent_invitations_rate.description: CheckInsNotOnSentInvitationsRate is count / check_in_count (0.0–1.0). Omitted when check_in_count == 0.

capacity_utilization.description: CapacityUtilization is check_in_count / room.capacity; may exceed 1.0 when overflow check-ins occurred. Omitted when capacity is 0.

overflow_rate.description: OverflowRate is overflow_count / check_in_count. Omitted when check_in_count is 0.

capacity_utilization.description: CapacityUtilization is check_in_count / room.capacity; may exceed 1.0 when overflow check-ins occurred. Omitted when capacity is 0.

overflow_rate.description: OverflowRate is overflow_count / check_in_count. Omitted when check_in_count is 0.

tags.description: Tags are the tags associated with this session. Each tag includes both its ID and name.

technical_difficulty.description: TechnicalDifficulty indicates how technical the session content is (snake_case JSON values).
